title: hebe speciosa
intro: Hebe speciosa, also known as the New Zealand hebe or coral bells, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the hebe family. The plant is used in traditional medicine, particularly in New Zealand, where it is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Hebe speciosa include its ability to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Herbal preparations of Hebe speciosa can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
